verdict.label: YELLOW · Half size, A-grade only
verdict.desc: The U.S.–Iran conflict is now in its tenth consecutive night of strikes, but reports that mediators are pushing a 10-day ceasefire eased oil and lifted risk appetite — S&P futures are up ~0.45% and chips keep recovering from last week’s rout. WTI settled ~$83 (up ~20% on the month) and is easing this morning; VIX sits near 18.2 and P(≥1% gap) is 33% — just under the 35% trigger, composite 50 (WARM). A live, open-ended war plus an oil shock lock GREEN out even with green futures: half size, A-grade only, keep DTE clear of the conflict window and the mega-cap prints (GOOGL/TSLA Wed 7/22).

focus.jbig: Iran conflict 10th night · oil eases toward $83 · 10-day ceasefire talk · half size
focus.jtag: Tenth straight night of U.S. strikes on Iran, but mediators push a 10-day ceasefire — oil eases, chips bounce, futures green
focus.jnote: Two forces still tug the tape, but the balance tilted toward relief overnight. The U.S. has now carried out its tenth consecutive night of strikes on Iran, and President Trump warned Tehran would “pay many times over” for the deaths of U.S. service members. But reports that mediators are pushing for a 10-day ceasefire helped ease oil in early trading after Monday’s rally, and semiconductor stocks kept recovering from last week’s selloff. WTI settled Monday around $83.23 (up ~20% on the month) and eased overnight; equity futures point higher (S&P +0.45%). Gold hovers near $4,020, still close to a nine-month low as the dollar stays firm and rate-hike fears persist. This is a live, headline-driven tape — deploy carefully, not aggressively.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~18.2, elevated; closed 18.65 Mon (−0.6%), 52-wk range 13.38–35.30, off the highs and easing
Big events (next 1–3 days) — live war headlines; GOOGL/TSLA/PM earnings Wed 7/22 AMC, INTC Thu; flash PMIs Thu, thin macro calendar
World risk — tenth straight night of U.S.–Iran strikes, U.S. deaths, Hormuz vessels intercepted; but 10-day ceasefire push easing panic
Volatility trend — front-month VIX near 3-month; curve sensitive to any Iran/oil shock, but diplomacy signal easing the spike
Commodities — oil up ~20% on the month, WTI ~$83 on Hormuz risk, easing on ceasefire talk; still the biggest single swing factor

snapshot.delta: Dow −0.59% Mon · Apple −2% dragged the Dow
settled $83.23 Mon · +20% on month, easing AM
near 2-mo high · oil lifts rate-hike bets

setups.read: The oil supply shock is real, durable news — crude is up ~20% on the month and energy keeps catching a supply-fear bid. But this is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper. The entire move is a conflict premium: mediators are now pushing a 10-day ceasefire, and one credible de-escalation headline gaps crude back $5–7 in minutes, taking your bull put the wrong way. No earnings issue, but the catalyst itself is the gap risk. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: On a YELLOW day, don’t chase a war premium. If you must, ½ size only after it’s clearly above open & prior close at 9:35, tight stop.

setups.read: Chips are bouncing again this morning, extending Monday’s recovery from last week’s brutal rout led by Samsung and TSMC in Asia. But a two-day bounce is not a durable catalyst — it’s a reflex rally, and GOOGL/TSLA report Wed 7/22 AMC, INTC Thu, dropping mega-cap AI-capex risk right into any DTE (INTC options price a ~15% swing). Selling premium into a stabilization attempt ahead of the season’s biggest prints is not a probability edge. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: A bounce isn’t a catalyst, and mega-cap earnings land Wed–Thu. Don’t underwrite a chip rebound into GOOGL/TSLA/INTC week.

setups.read: Jet fuel remains elevated with crude near $83, a direct margin headwind — so the fundamental lean is down. But two problems: a ceasefire/diplomacy headline squeezes shorts violently (mediators are pushing a 10-day truce now), and VIX near 18 makes any bear call a snap-back hazard. A bear call into a live-war, VIX-elevated tape with active peace talks is exactly the blow-up 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: Diplomacy-squeeze risk into a fuel headwind = wait. Don’t chase the short until the tape settles.

setups.read: No clean fresh post-earnings gap this Tuesday morning that qualifies. Domino’s reported Monday and GM is due pre-bell today, but the mega-cap wave (Alphabet, Tesla, PM Wed; Intel, IBM, T-Mobile Thu) hasn’t hit yet. Nothing has reported-and-gapped cleanly in the last 24–48h into a war tape. Check back Thursday once the big prints land.
setups.check_note: Nothing to validate — no Type A candidate today.

setups.read: A war-headline, oil-shock tape is a poor backdrop for clean breakouts, even with futures green. The oil refiners that hit highs mid-July are war-premium moves outside their 4-session windows, and no fresh high-volume ATH breakout is holding into an Iran-strike, pre-mega-cap-earnings open. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid tape; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Relief attempt on ceasefire talk, still headline-driven
scenarios.sub: Futures hold green, chips stabilize, oil eases on the 10-day ceasefire push; but tape stays jumpy on any Iran/oil headline, vol elevated

scenarios.label: Bad case — Escalation / Hormuz disruption
scenarios.sub: Ceasefire talk collapses or Iran hits a base; oil runs back toward $90–95, equities gap down, VIX pops

scenarios.label: Good case — Diplomacy breakthrough
scenarios.sub: The 10-day ceasefire firms up; oil gives back the spike, relief rally in stocks and chips, vol eases hard

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure / wider war
scenarios.sub: A strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Iran / Strait of Hormuz — the U.S. has now struck Iran for ten consecutive nights and U.S. service members have been killed; Tehran intercepted vessels in the strait over the weekend. Any wider strike or a collapse of the 10-day ceasefire push sends crude and jet fuel vertical — the week’s biggest tail.

Diplomacy whipsaw — mediators are pushing a 10-day ceasefire and Iran’s Foreign Ministry says negotiations could continue. A breakthrough would gap oil and war-trades violently the other way — brutal for anyone short the squeeze or long the premium.

Oil near $83 & the 10-year — WTI up ~20% on the month keeps inflation risk alive despite cooler June CPI/PPI; the 10Y sits near ~4.59%, a two-month high, and markets price ~53% odds of a September Fed hike.

Mega-cap earnings · GOOGL & TSLA (Wed 7/22 AMC), INTC (Thu) — the first Magnificent Seven prints; INTC options imply a ~15% swing and Tesla has fallen after three of its last four reports. Any AI-capex surprise could reignite the chip selloff. Keep spreads clear of each name’s print.
Flash PMIs & jobless claims (Thu 7/24) — macro catalysts stacked on top of the war and mega-cap earnings. A weak PMI or hot claims print would compound the risk.

earnings.intro: Q2 season goes mega-cap this week — Alphabet, Tesla and Philip Morris headline Wednesday 7/22 after the close, with Intel, IBM, T-Mobile and RTX on Thursday. With the Iran conflict still live and oil elevated, a single-stock earnings gap on top of headline risk is a double catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print.

rows.why: Cloud growth and AI-capex guidance are the swing factors — the market watches Alphabet’s cloud backlog and monetization for the whole tech tape. Beats have topped expectations 13 straight quarters and the stock averages a ~1.3% advance on report days. First mega-cap read of the season.
rows.angle: Do not hold a GOOGL spread through Wednesday’s print. A clean beat-and-gap can set up a Type A into Thursday.

rows.why: Record 480K deliveries, but focus is on automotive margins, FSD/AI progress and guidance. The stock fell ~6.6% last week and has dropped after three of its last four reports (including an 8.2% slide). High-beta gap risk into a jumpy war tape.
rows.angle: Do not carry a TSLA spread through Wednesday. A miss-and-gap-down becomes a Type A bear-call setup next session.

rows.why: Intel is down ~13% on the week in the chip selloff; consensus ~$0.22 EPS on ~$14.4B revenue (up ~12% y/y) with a ~15% implied swing. IBM already posted prelim figures that hit the stock. Any AI-capex or second-half surprise could reignite the memory rout — stacking single-stock risk on the live Iran backdrop.
rows.angle: Keep any tech spread to a DTE that clears the earnings date — and mind the still-live geopolitical tail plus the PMI overlap.

earnings.outro: Tue 7/21: GM pre-bell plus off-cycle names; the tape trades the Iran/oil headlines and the chip bounce. It gets real Wednesday 7/22 with mega-cap tech (Alphabet, Tesla) after the close, then Intel/IBM/T-Mobile Thursday alongside flash PMIs and jobless claims. Half size, A-grade only, until the tape settles.
